steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for an introduction to Walker’s career aimed at readers who already know MBC. That means the answer must: (1) name Walker as a notable figure within MBC, and (2) avoid re-explaining what MBC is or padding with irrelevant details. It should open the door to a discussion of Walker specifically.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘While George Theophilus Walker is considered a great composer, there are many more such composers included in MBC’s Historic Composers Directory.’: buries Walker’s significance by pivoting immediately to “many more,” which undercuts him as a subject. Shifts focus away from Walker rather than setting up his career. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Born in 1922, George Theophilus Walker passed away in 2018 according to the MBC project.’: gives only biographical dates with no context about why Walker matters or what follows. Too thin to set up a career discussion. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Among the many talented figures included in the MBC project is classical music composer George Theophilus Walker.’: situates Walker within MBC (which the reader already knows) and identifies him as a classical music composer, creating the right launching point for a career discussion. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘The MBC project’s Historic Composers Directory highlights Black classical composers of the past…’: re-explains MBC’s mission to an audience that already knows it. Uses Walker only as an example, not as the subject. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
